title: Sokoban (learned from play)
discoveries: Player+Target and Crate+Target share cells, but Player and Crate never do, so Target lives on its own collision layer while Player/Crate/Wall share one.
Pushing works: A1 DOWN moves Player onto the crate's old cell while the crate slides one down, confirming a single push rule.
Walls block: A2 LEFT (into a wall) and A6 repeated DOWN into a wall leave the grid unchanged.
The Crate+Target cell stays fixed and the player can occupy a Target (A7 shows Player+Target), so Target is a passive overlay object.
No logged attempt wins; two crates and two targets (one already satisfied) suggest "All Crate on Target" as the win condition.
The prior crash was purely a missing "." = Background legend entry, not a logic error.
